source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure alerts are enabled for vulnerabilities discovered in container images in Container Registry

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure alerts are enabled for vulnerabilities discovered in container images in Container Registry
input_row.description: With IBM Cloud™ Security Advisor, you can instantly view the security posture of your IBM Cloud services through a single, centralized dashboard. The service receives security information from various sources and displays any security alerts or vulnerabilities that require your attention in the service dashboard. Out of the box, there are several pre-populated cards in your dashboard. These findings are from security services in IBM Cloud, but you can also add cards or custom partner solutions so that all of your security tools can be accessed from the same location. Through pre-integrated findings, you can monitor: • Certificates that you manage with IBM Cloud Certificate Manager • Vulnerabilities in container images that are stored in IBM Cloud Container Registry Monitoring vulnerabilities in container images With Container Registry, you have access to Vulnerability Advisor, which continuously scans the images in your Container Registry instance for potential security issues. If issues are found, you are alerted and can view a comprehensive report in your Security Advisor dashboard. Enable alerts for critical/high/medium vulnerabilities discovered in container images in Container Registry to ensure that workload vulnerabilities are addressed in a timely manner and not deployed to a production environment.
input_row.rationale: Security Insights in the IBM Cloud Security and Compliance Center offers notification alerts in Vulnerability Advisor to identify vulnerabilities in developer owned and base container images which are often overlooked. This feature alerts for vulnerabilities in images used explicitly and implicitly. It is recommended alerts for critical/high/medium vulnerable container images in Container Registry be enabled for every image. Impact: A significant percentage of data breaches are caused by users who have forgotten to update their dependency and runs a more vulnerable version in production. If the control of alerts is not utilized, vulnerable images will likely be written into production more often than desired and will quickly be vulnerable to a cyberattack. With Security Advisor alerts in place, you are notified and can start resolving issues immediately. Before you begin Before you get started with alerts, you must have the following prerequisites: • The Manager IAM role. • A configured webhook. If you don't have one already, try using Cloud Functions.
input_row.audit: Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Click Menu icon > Security and Compliance . 3. In the Gain insight section of the navigation, click Configure-->Alerts . 4. Verify that at least one channel is listed and is set to Enabled .
input_row.remediation: All remediation steps must be applied to the vulnerability in the container image by image owners. Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Click Menu icon > Security and Compliance . 3. In the Gain insight section of the navigation, click Configure-->Alerts . 4. Click Create channel . 5. Provide a Name , optional Description , and Channel endpoint . 6. Under Severity for notifications , Select Critical , High , and Medium 7. Click Save Default Value: By default, Vulnerability Advisor alerts are not configured for container images in Container Registry. References: 1. https://nvd.nist.gov/800-53/Rev4/control/RA-5 2. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/security-advisor?topic=security-advisor-getting- started 3. https://cloud.ibm.com/security-advisor#/notifications
input_row.impact: A significant percentage of data breaches are caused by users who have forgotten to update their dependency and runs a more vulnerable version in production. If the control of alerts is not utilized, vulnerable images will likely be written into production more often than desired and will quickly be vulnerable to a cyberattack. With Security Advisor alerts in place, you are notified and can start resolving issues immediately. Before you begin Before you get started with alerts, you must have the following prerequisites: • The Manager IAM role. • A configured webhook. If you don't have one already, try using Cloud Functions.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control to ensure alerts are enabled for vulnerabilities discovered in container images in the IBM Cloud Container Registry can be audited through automated processes. This control involves checking configurations related to the IBM Cloud Security Advisor and the Vulnerability Advisor, which can be queried and configured using IBM Cloud APIs and CLI commands.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_container_registry_vulnerability_alerts_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **IBM Cloud CLI Commands:** Use the IBM Cloud CLI to verify that alert channels are configured and enabled for container image vulnerabilities.
- **API Calls:** Utilize the IBM Cloud Security Advisor and Container Registry APIs to check for the existence of alert configurations for vulnerabilities.
- **Configuration Properties to Validate:**
  - Ensure that alert channels for critical, high, and medium severity vulnerabilities are enabled.
  - Verify that a valid channel endpoint is configured in the IBM Cloud Security Advisor for receiving vulnerability alerts.
- **Resources to Enumerate:**
  - All container registry instances to check if vulnerability alerts are configured for each.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** Alerts for critical, high, and medium vulnerabilities are enabled, and a valid endpoint is configured.
  - **Fail:** If alerts are not configured or if they cover fewer than the critical, high, and medium severity levels, or if no endpoint is configured.

This process ensures that the alerts for container image vulnerabilities are properly configured and can be audited consistently across IBM Cloud environments. The use of IBM Cloud APIs and CLI ensures the audit can be automated, aligning with industry-standard CSPM tools.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively audited using IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI commands. IBM Cloud provides robust API and CLI support for managing and querying configurations related to the Security Advisor and Container Registry, making automation feasible.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. IBM Cloud CLI commands and API calls can be used to verify alert configurations for vulnerabilities in container images. However, the specific CLI commands and API endpoints should be explicitly mentioned for clarity.
- The program name follows the convention but could be more descriptive. It currently lacks specificity regarding the severity levels being checked (e.g., 'critical', 'high', 'medium').

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Specific Commands and API Endpoints:** The assessment should include specific IBM Cloud CLI commands and API endpoints used to check alert configurations. For example, using `ibmcloud cr va` for vulnerability assessments and `ibmcloud sa` for Security Advisor configurations.
- **Program Name:** A more descriptive program name could be `ibm_container_registry_vulnerability_alerts_critical_high_medium_enabled` to clearly indicate the severity levels being audited.
- **Additional Considerations:** Consider verifying the integration of alert channels with external systems (e.g., Slack, email) to ensure alerts are actionable. Additionally, ensure that the audit checks for any recent updates or changes in the IBM Cloud API that might affect the audit process.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- I am confident in the assessment's approach and technical details, given the capabilities of IBM Cloud APIs and CLI for automating such checks.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Include specific IBM Cloud CLI commands and API endpoints in the automation details for clarity and completeness. Update the program name to be more descriptive regarding the severity levels being audited.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that IBM Cloud provides API and CLI support for querying and verifying alert configurations in the Container Registry and Security Advisor. The audit steps described in the control can be replicated programmatically by listing and inspecting alert channels and their severity settings via IBM Cloud CLI or API. This allows for a reliable, repeatable, and scalable automated audit.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
ibm_container_registry_alert_channels_critical_high_medium_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- IBM Cloud Resource/Service: Container Registry (Vulnerability Advisor), Security Advisor Alert Channels
- API/CLI Method:
  - Use IBM Cloud CLI: `ibmcloud cr va` for vulnerability advisor settings.
  - Use IBM Cloud CLI: `ibmcloud sa notification-channel-list` to list alert channels.
  - IBM Cloud Security Advisor API: `GET /v3/notification/channels` to enumerate channels and their configuration.
- Properties to Check:
  - At least one alert channel exists and is enabled.
  - The channel is configured to alert on "Critical", "High", and "Medium" severities.
  - The channel has a valid endpoint (e.g., email, webhook, Slack, etc.).
- Pass Criteria:
  - There is at least one enabled alert channel for container image vulnerabilities, configured for "Critical", "High", and "Medium" severities, with a valid endpoint.
- Fail Criteria:
  - No alert channels exist, or none are enabled.
  - Channels do not cover all three severities ("Critical", "High", "Medium").
  - No valid endpoint is configured.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name and explicit CLI/API references, which have been incorporated for clarity and precision.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
